summarization.a: In-plane XRD (2θχ) for 200-cycle Li-MnO₂ sample: The main reflections correspond to the spinel phase LiMn₂O₄ at (111), (131), (222), (440), and (351). An impurity peak, marked with an asterisk (*), is also visible.
summarization.b: Out-of-plane XRD (θ–2θ) for 200-cycle Li-MnO₂ sample: Shows dominant (111) and (311) reflections for the lithiated spinel phase, along with remaining MnO₂ (100, 200) reflections. The diagram illustrates the orientation of grains relative to the substrate.
summarization.c: In-plane XRD (2θχ) for 300-cycle Li-MnO₂ sample: Similar LiMn₂O₄ reflections as in (a) are present, confirming spinel formation after 300 cycles. Impurity peaks may still be detectable.
summarization.d: Out-of-plane XRD (θ–2θ) for 300-cycle Li-MnO₂ sample: Shows stronger LiMn₂O₄ peaks compared to (b) and reduced MnO₂ reflections, indicating progression toward complete conversion. The impurity peak (*) is also marked. This figure collectively demonstrates the transformation from β-MnO₂ to LiMn₂O₄ with increasing Li-cycling, while highlighting residual precursor and minor impurity phases.

d.question: Why is the removal of the Li₂₋ₓMnO₂ impurity and the growth of sharp, well-defined spinel peaks a critical step for producing a high-performance cathode film?
d.answer: Eliminating impurities and achieving high crystallinity ensures uniform electrochemical properties, stable lithium-ion pathways, and minimal parasitic reactions. This directly translates to higher capacity, better rate capability, and longer cycle life in a lithium-ion battery.

a.question: Based on the XRD patterns in this figure , what is the primary effect of annealing at 600°C on the 200cLi-MnO₂ and 300cLi-MnO₂ films?
a.answer: Annealing significantly sharpens and intensifies the LiMn₂O₄ spinel peaks while reducing the broad, disordered background. This indicates enhanced crystallinity and improved long-range atomic order within the films.

a.question: Compare the XRD peak sharpening effect of annealing between the 200cLi and 300cLi samples. Which film undergoes a more dramatic improvement in crystallinity, and why might this be?
a.answer: The 300cLi sample shows more dramatic peak sharpening. This is likely because it starts closer to the stoichiometric LiMn₂O₄ composition, allowing annealing to more efficiently drive the crystallization of the phase-pure spinel structure.

a.question: Observing the impurity phase marked with '*', how does its signal change after annealing the 200cLi and 300cLi samples? What does this suggest about the thermal stability of this phase?
a.answer: The impurity phase (Li₂₋ₓMnO₂) is present in the unannealed samples but is eliminated after annealing. This indicates it is a metastable intermediate that decomposes or transforms into the more thermodynamically stable LiMn₂O₄ spinel phase upon heating.
